name: webflow-automation
description: Automate Webflow CMS collections, site publishing, page management, asset uploads, and ecommerce orders via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: List, create, update, and delete Webflow CMS collection items with proper schema validation
Publish Webflow sites to production with custom domain or subdomain targeting
Upload assets (images, files) with base64 encoding and MD5 integrity verification
List and inspect site pages including DOM structure retrieval
Retrieve ecommerce order data with status filtering
Navigate Webflow's 24-character hexadecimal ID system for sites, collections, items, and pages
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer detected 158 external command patterns and 1 network reference. All external command detections are FALSE POSITIVES - they identify MCP tool names in documentation (e.g., WEBFLOW_LIST_WEBFLOW_SITES), not actual shell execution. The network finding references the legitimate Rube MCP endpoint (rube.app/mcp) required for skill operation. MD5 usage is for file integrity verification during S3 uploads, not cryptographic security. No malicious intent detected. Skill is safe for publication with minor documentation warnings about production actions.
valueStatement: Managing Webflow content manually is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates CMS operations, site publishing, and asset management through Rube MCP integration with Composio's Webflow toolkit.
userTitle: Automate Webflow CMS and Site Operations
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Webflow OAuth authentication
Different OAuth scopes required for different operations (sites:read, cms:write, etc.)
Site publishing is rate-limited to 1 publish per minute
File uploads require base64-encoded content and manually computed MD5 hash

useCases.targetUser: Content managers and marketing operations teams
useCases.title: Content Manager Bulk CMS Operations
useCases.description: Marketing teams can批量 create blog posts, update product listings, or manage team member directories by calling CMS tools in sequence with proper field slug resolution.

useCases.targetUser: Web developers managing client Webflow sites
useCases.title: Developer Site Deployment Workflow
useCases.description: Developers can automate site publishing after staged changes, verify page structures via DOM inspection, and manage multiple site environments programmatically.

useCases.title: Ecommerce Order Monitoring
useCases.description: Store operators can retrieve order data, filter by status, and integrate order information into external reporting or fulfillment workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: List Available Webflow Sites
promptTemplates.prompt: List all Webflow sites I have access to and show me their IDs and names.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with Webflow automation to discover available sites

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: View CMS Collections
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me all CMS collections for the site with ID [site_id] and explain what each collection contains.
promptTemplates.scenario: Exploring existing CMS structure before making changes

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Create Blog Post
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new blog post in the blog-posts collection with title "[Title]", slug "[post-slug]", and content "[content]". First verify the collection schema to get correct field slugs.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding new CMS content with proper schema validation

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Complete CMS Workflow with Publishing
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to add 3 new team members to our team collection. For each person, create a CMS item with their name, role, bio, and headshot. Then publish the site to make changes live. Confirm with me before publishing.
promptTemplates.scenario: Multi-step CMS operations culminating in production deployment

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before any Webflow operations
Call GET_COLLECTION to retrieve field slugs before creating or updating CMS items - never guess field names
Confirm with users before executing PUBLISH_SITE or DELETE_COLLECTION_ITEM as these are irreversible production actions
antiPatterns: Using field display names instead of slugs in CMS operations - always retrieve schema first
Publishing without reviewing staged changes - PUBLISH_SITE deploys ALL pending changes
Assuming all sites have ecommerce enabled - verify before calling order-related endpoints

faq.question: How do I connect Webflow to this skill?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client. Then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit "webflow". If not active, follow the auth link to complete OAuth.

faq.question: Why are my CMS changes not appearing on my live site?
faq.answer: CMS operations stage changes but don't publish them. Call PUBLISH_SITE with appropriate domain targets to deploy changes to production.

faq.question: What is the difference between field slugs and display names?
faq.answer: Display names are human-readable labels shown in Webflow UI. Slugs are URL-safe identifiers used in API calls. Always use slugs (e.g., "author-name") in field_data, not display names (e.g., "Author Name").

faq.question: Why do I get 403 errors when creating CMS items?
faq.answer: Your OAuth connection may lack required scopes. CMS write operations need cms:write scope. Re-authenticate with expanded permissions if needed.

faq.question: Can I upload images directly from URLs?
faq.answer: No. The UPLOAD_ASSET endpoint requires base64-encoded file_content and manually computed MD5 hash. You must first download and encode the image data.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination for large collections?
faq.answer: Use offset and limit parameters on LIST_COLLECTION_ITEMS and LIST_PAGES. Increment offset by limit until returned results are fewer than the limit.
